regime_note: Moving from CRUDE_SHOCK toward NEUTRAL. WTI dropped from $93.70 to $89 (-4%). Gold diverging upward (safe haven demand separate from energy). Yen strengthening. Dollar weakening.

open_positions.thesis: Gold broke day high ($4,817.75) while WTI dumps -4.4%. Divergence signals macro hedging demand beyond energy fear. Fresh breakout with geopolitical backdrop (Hormuz tense, dollar weak). R:R 2.38:1.
open_positions.trailing_note: Activate trailing stop when Gold hits $4,859 (50% of TP distance). Until then, keep SL at $4,785.
open_positions.management_rules: Do NOT move SL to breakeven yet — too early (only 12% to TP). Normal pullbacks of $10-15 are expected in a $90 daily range. Only tighten stop after $4,859.

watchlist.size_plan: Calculate via risk_guard at entry time
watchlist.thesis: Yen safe haven bid accelerating — USD/JPY down -0.42% and grinding lower. Aligns with Gold strength (both risk-off). Day low 158.598 is the trigger. Break = fresh momentum.
watchlist.invalidation: If USD/JPY bounces above 159.00, cancel this setup.

watchlist.trigger: Bounce to $91-92 zone (sell the bounce, don't chase the dump)
watchlist.entry_plan: Short at ~$91.50 on retest of broken support
watchlist.thesis: WTI dropped 4%+ today. De-escalation pricing. But don't short at $89 after a 5% dump — wait for a bounce to $91-92 where old support becomes resistance.
watchlist.invalidation: If WTI reclaims $93 (today's high), thesis is dead — Hormuz re-escalation.

watchlist.trigger: Break above $76,100 (today's high) — NOT active, momentum fading
watchlist.thesis: BTC was +3% earlier but now fading to +2.16%. Momentum lost. Only enter if fresh breakout above day high. Otherwise skip.
watchlist.invalidation: If BTC drops below $73,000 (today's low), setup is dead.

watchlist.thesis: Dollar weakness building (+0.34%). EUR/USD creeping toward day high. Break above 1.1811 = confirmed dollar sell. Aligns with Gold strength and yen bid.

key_events_upcoming: Apr 15: India market reopens (F&O expiry + Reliance Q4)
market_context.hormuz_status: Blockade ongoing, ceasefire nominally holds to Apr 22. IRGC attacked ships Apr 12-13. WTI pulling back suggests market pricing de-escalation.
market_context.dominant_theme: De-escalation pricing (WTI dump) BUT safe haven demand persists (Gold bid, yen bid). Market is hedging tail risk while base-casing resolution.
